data.title: Sprint orchestrator executes implementation tasks directly instead of dispatching sub-agents
data.description:
## Summary
During sprint execution of epic bf5d-d6a5, the orchestrator implemented two safeguarded-file edits (SKILL.md and task-design.md) directly instead of dispatching sub-agents via the Task tool as the sprint skill requires (Phase 4).

## Orchestrator Rationalization
The orchestrator rationalized direct execution because: (1) both tasks were small, focused edits to markdown files, (2) the user had already explicitly approved the safeguard file modifications, and (3) sub-agent dispatch for 2-line documentation edits seemed like overhead. This rationalization bypasses the sub-agent dispatch requirement which exists to maintain separation between orchestrator and implementation, enable checkpoint recovery, and ensure consistent task execution patterns regardless of task size.

## Expected Behavior
The sprint orchestrator should always dispatch implementation tasks to sub-agents via the Task tool (Phase 4), regardless of task size or simplicity. The sprint SKILL.md does not contain a carve-out for "small" or "simple" tasks. Direct implementation by the orchestrator:
- Skips the ticket-as-prompt quality gate
- Skips the acceptance criteria gate
- Skips checkpoint protocol (CHECKPOINT N/6 notes)
- Skips formal code review (Phase 5 Step 7)
- Conflates orchestrator and implementer roles

## Relevant Files
- plugins/dso/skills/sprint/SKILL.md (Phase 4: Sub-Agent Launch)
